text: Patient is a 28-year-old male that presented this morning on account of fever and headache of two days' duration. Fever was low-grade and intermittent. The headache was generalised, aching in nature, graded 8/10 and non-radiating. There was associated symptoms of chills, generalised body pain, bitter tastes in mouth. There was no history of cough, vomiting, passage of loose stool, dysuria or discharge from craniofacial orifices. Patient had visited a pharmacy yesterday, where he was administered anti-malaria medications and paracetamol but there has been no relief. He is not a known hypertensive, diabetic, epileptic or sickle cell patient. There is no history of any significant hospital admission, blood transfusion or surgical procedures. He drinks alcohol occasionally, but does not smoke cigarettes or keep recreational drugs. On examination, young male, acutely ill looking, febrile, not pale, not dehydrated, anicteric. Diagnosis malaria, R/O bacterial infection.
